items.title: Wanner kreeg zes maanden van Bosz en maakte daarin indruk
items.summary: PSV-trainer Peter Bosz weet dat het lastig kan zijn om vanuit Duitsland voor het eerst naar het buitenland te gaan en dus gaf hij Paul Wanner zes maanden de tijd om te wennen aan Eindhoven en het spel van PSV. In die missie is de ex-speler van Bayern München meer dan geslaagd volgens Bosz.

items.title: Ipswich brengt vergeefs bod uit op Piroe, Slot rekent op Robertson
items.summary: De tijd van winterse versterkingen is aangebroken, in dit blog volgen we alle transferactiviteit in het buitenland. Bekijk hier ons blog voor de transferperikelen in Nederland.

items.title: Mou bids Arbeloa success -- except vs. Benfica
items.summary: Benfica coach José Mourinho wishes his former player Álvaro Arbeloa success as coach of Real Madrid except when they square off Wednesday in Lisbon in the Champions League.

items.title: Slot expects Robertson to stay at Liverpool
items.summary: Arne Slot has said he expects defender Andrew Robertson to stay at Liverpool for the rest of the season despite interest from Tottenham Hotspur.

items.title: Ex-FIFA prez backs call to 'stay away' from WC
items.summary: Former FIFA president Sepp Blatter on Monday backed a proposed fan boycott of World Cup matches in the United States because of the conduct of President Donald Trump and his administration at home and abroad.

items.title: Arteta feeling bullish after Arsenal ‘take temperature down’ with team meeting
items.summary: Head coach and players spoke in wake of United defeat Side is ‘in great position in four competitions’, he says Mikel Arteta has revealed Arsenal held a team meeting after their defeat against Manchester United on Sunday “to take the temperature down”, insisting the league leaders must “play with enjoyment” in order to win a first Premier League title for 22 years. A late goal from Matheus Cunha inflicted on Arsenal their first home defeat of the season, with their lead at the top of the table remaining at four points after weekend victories for Manchester City and Aston Villa. Arteta’s side have finished as runners-up in the past three seasons and Arsenal have now spent 884 days at the summit since Arsène Wenger’s Invincibles were crowned champions in 2004. Continue reading...

items.title: De Graafschap-banneling voltooit transfer naar League Two-deelnemer
items.summary: Na Kelly N'Mai, Sil Swinkels en Ismael Kabia kent de Engelse League Two voortaan een vierde Nederlander: Bristol City heeft de komst van Mees Rijks dinsdag wereldkundig gemaakt. Zo komt er een einde aan Rijks' uitleenbeurt bij De Graafschap, waar hij niet meer in de plannen voorkwam.

items.title: The Premier League stars who could still move this transfer window
items.summary: There is less than a week left of the January transfer window. Here are the Premier League players who could relocate.

items.title: Transfer rumors, news: Cole Palmer open to Man United move
items.summary: Cole Palmer, who is reportedly homesick in London, is open to a move to Manchester United. Transfer Talk has the latest.

items.title: 'Jump on the fun boat' - Arteta appeals to Arsenal fans
items.summary: Arsenal boss Mikel Arteta promises "beautiful" things that "we cannot even imagine" lie ahead after gathering his players together following their home defeat by Manchester United.

items.title: Slot weet welk probleem hij moet oplossen: 'Dan wordt het seizoen nog speciaal'
items.summary: Arne Slot heeft het seizoen met Liverpool nog zeker niet opgegeven. De Nederlandse manager weet precies welke twee problemen zijn ploeg moet oplossen. 'Als dat lukt, kunnen we zeker nog een prijs pakken.'

items.title: Newcastle latest: Joelinton out for weeks as Bruno faces fitness test
West Ham consider loaning Kinsky and Disasi as Bournemouth confirm Rayan deal
items.summary: Igor Julio must return to Brighton if West Ham are to act Bournemouth sign Rayan from Vasco da Gama for £24.7m West Ham are considering loan moves for Tottenham’s Antonin Kinsky and Chelsea’s Axel Disasi but will need to send Igor Julio back to Brighton if both moves are to go through. Nuno Espírito Santo has strengthened his attack by signing Taty Castellanos and Pablo Felipe for a package worth up to £47m and is looking to boost his side in their battle against relegation by bringing in a goalkeeper and a defender. Continue reading...

items.title: Slot says Liverpool must fix ‘imbalance’ to silence growing outside noise
items.summary: Arne Slot has admitted Liverpool must improve their play at both ends of the pitch if they want to put a stop to recent criticism.

items.title: Mourinho goedgemutst richting weerzien met 'een van mijn favoriete spelers'
items.summary: Woensdagavond staat er een echte Europese klassieker op het programma in Lissabon: Benfica – Real Madrid. José Mourinho staat tegenover zijn oude club, en in de Madrileense dug-out zit met Álvaro Arbeloa iemand voor wie de Special One veel respect heeft.

items.title: Arteta encouraged by 'beautiful' meeting with players in wake of Man Utd loss
items.summary: Mikel Arteta has revealed he called his players for a meeting to "bring down the temperature" - and pleaded with Arsenal's anxious fanbase to "jump on the fun boat" - in the wake of their defeat to Manchester United.

items.title: Bayern legend? How Kane is viewed in Germany as contract talks begin
items.summary: Harry Kane is entering the final 18 months of his Bayern Munich contract, and the German champions are eager to keep the prolific striker, as German football writer Constantin Eckner explains.

items.title: Football Daily | Harry Kane and the trolls that take aim at his staggering success
items.summary: Sign up now! Sign up now! Sign up now? Sign up now! The most prolific striker on the planet for one of the biggest and most successful clubs in the world, a captain of his country who could possibly lift the World Cup this summer (ending what is now 60 years of hurt), a husband with four kids who is almost universally respected by fans, opponents and his peers, and a brand ambassador for global brands thanks to deals brokered by towering Mr 15% Charlie Kane, Harry Kane has a lot going for him. So why has the England superstar been such a target for Social Media Disgrace trolling in January? The world is indeed a screwed up place when Sepp Blatter’s utterings start making sense. Oh Gianni, what have you done?” – Krishna Moorthy. Big Website’s Michael Butler misses the point about his stay in the Radisson Hotel, Blackpool. The hotel’s strategy is to provide its customers with a pleasant experience undisturbed by the goings on outside the bedroom window. They don’t want bad reviews from customers forced to watch the match” – Deryck Hall (who may be a Preston fan). We are now very accustomed to seeing many, many minutes of additional time being added on to the old-fashioned ‘90’ that we all grew up with … but last weekend the free-climber Alex Honnold needed only one minute of ‘Fergie time’ to complete his ‘look no hands!’ climb of Taipei 101 without the aid of a safety net, parachute or indeed anything more sophisticated than a bag of chalk dust. Footballers, take note” – Allastair McGillivray. Continue reading...

items.title: Palmer 'untouchable' at Chelsea amid Man Utd links
items.summary: Chelsea consider Cole Palmer "untouchable" amid links with Manchester United.

items.title: Guardiola apologises to ref Hallam but 'defends' outburst
items.summary: Pep Guardiola has apologised to new Premier League referee Farai Hallam if he "feels offended", having taken issue with the official following Manchester City's victory over Wolves on Saturday. But the Spaniard said he has to "defend my club and my players".
